criteria.name: keyboard_reaches_everything
criteria.description: Sidebar entries, toolbar and macro controls, grid rows, queue move controls, retry and pause/resume controls, export and import controls, and all form fields are reachable and operable with Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, and Space
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: modals_manage_focus
criteria.description: The composer, schedule form, export drawer, import surface, and confirmation dialogs trap focus while open, close on Escape, and return focus to the control that opened them
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: icons_have_labels
criteria.description: Status icons, macro icons, and row-action icons carry accessible names or labels
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: run_events_announced
criteria.description: Run completion, an item entering the failed state, and pause/resume transitions are announced through an aria-live region as well as shown visually
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: forms_have_explicit_labels
criteria.description: Composer and schedule fields have visible labels, and validation messages are programmatically associated so each names its field
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: headings_follow_order
criteria.description: Panel and section headings follow a logical order with no skips
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: landmarks_present
criteria.description: Landmark structure (nav, main) or a skip link is present
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: contrast_sufficient
criteria.description: Status colors, badge text, and rollup values meet sufficient contrast against their backgrounds
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: semantic_roles_used
criteria.description: Interactive elements use semantic roles — real buttons for macros and retries, a table or grid role for the execution grid
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: reduced_motion_respected
criteria.description: Animations respect prefers-reduced-motion: rows, statuses, and the bar apply instantly with flows intact
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: keyboard_queue_reorder
criteria.description: The pending queue is fully reorderable with the keyboard move controls alone, with focus staying on the moved entry
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: diff_not_color_only
criteria.description: The inspector's expected-versus-actual comparison and item statuses are distinguishable by more than color alone (icons, markers, or text labels)
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: multi_facet_seeded_reset
criteria.description: Multi-facet round-trip: create a job, reorder its queue, set a schedule, and apply a timeline filter, then reload; every facet coherently resets to the seeded baseline (seeded jobs and history, no schedules, default filter) — never a mix of kept and reset state
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: queue_order_round_trip
criteria.description: Queue-order proof: move a pending item down two slots and then back up two; the queue returns to its exact original order, and the launch order follows whichever order is current, proving order derives from live state
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: rollups_track_step_states
criteria.description: Derived-view sensitivity: as items fail, retry, and complete during a run, the failure rate, n of m, and accumulated cost in the rollup strip change accordingly — never an identical redraw regardless of item states
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: cross_view_echo_retry
criteria.description: Cross-view echo: resolving a failed item via Retry failed items updates the grid row, the event timeline, the run history's outcome counts, and the open inspector without a reload
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: count_delta_exact
criteria.description: Count-delta integrity: composing a job increases the sidebar job count by exactly one, and each launch adds exactly one run history entry, measured immediately before and after
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: two_launches_differ_deterministically
criteria.description: Input-dependent output with deterministic failures: two launches of the same job produce different simulated outputs and latencies, while the set of first-attempt-failing items is identical both times
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: interleaved_flows_intact
criteria.description: Interleaved-flow integrity: begin composing a new job, switch to a running job and pause it, return and finish composing; the new job appears correctly and the paused run's checkpoint is intact
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: empty_to_repopulated_round_trip
criteria.description: Edge-state round-trip: delete every job to reach the empty state, then compose a new job; the sidebar, main panel, and count all track through both transitions
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: durability_export_pipeline
criteria.description: Full pipeline probe: launch the large job, let failures retry, retry the exhausted item, pause and resume, then open Export run; the JSON shows schemaVersion batch-run-v1 and its job fields, per-item statuses, attempts, costs, and rollups match the on-screen grid and rollup strip exactly, including every session mutation
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: stop_all_export_truthful
criteria.description: Macro truthfulness: after Stop All, the grid marks pending and running items stopped, completed outputs are retained, and Export run then records those items with status stopped — not complete
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: export_import_reconstructs_run
criteria.description: Export/import pipeline: mutate a run (retry or Stop All), Copy or Download the Run Report JSON, Import that same text, and confirm the visible grid, rollups, and timeline match the exported document and the next Export preview matches again
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: seeded_jobs_and_sidebar
criteria.description: On load, the left sidebar lists at least 3 seeded jobs — at least 2 completed jobs with at least 30 rows each and 1 Ready job with at least 200 rows — and each entry shows the job name, created date, row count, and current status
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: composer_fields_complete
criteria.description: Clicking New Job opens a modal whose create-job fields include name, a promptTemplate selector offering at least 5 seeded templates, a model selector offering exactly the 4 seeded models (atlas-40b, lyra-8b, quasar-mini, helix-2) each shown with its per-1,000-token rate, and a concurrency control accepting 1 through 5 with a default of 3
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: dataset_preview_and_count
criteria.description: In the composer, both picking a seeded dataset slice and pasting JSON-array or CSV text render a preview table with columns mapped from the detected fields and a row count badge matching the number of detected rows; a dataset with an expected-output field is noted as including expected outputs
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: composer_validation_gating
criteria.description: The composer's Submit control stays disabled until name, prompt template, and model are valid and at least one dataset row is detected; submitting with zero rows shows an inline message naming the dataset field and creates no job, and pasting malformed JSON or CSV shows an inline parse error and commits no rows
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: valid_submit_creates_job
criteria.description: Submitting a valid composer form closes the modal, adds exactly one job to the sidebar in a Ready state, and increases the visible job count by exactly one
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: execution_grid_columns
criteria.description: Launching a Ready job renders a per-item execution grid with one row per dataset row showing row index, input summary (first 60 characters), status, attempt count, latency in milliseconds, and accumulated item cost
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: status_progression_visible
criteria.description: Item statuses advance visibly through pending, running, and complete or failed during a real launch, and a retrying item shows a retrying status with its attempt counter
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: concurrency_guard_enforced
criteria.description: At no moment do more items than the job's configured concurrency limit show the running state, and the concurrency guard strip shows the live running count against the limit and takes an amber treatment while saturated
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: deterministic_failures_backoff
criteria.description: A subset of items fails on first attempt with a visible backoff countdown and attempt counter (such as waiting before retry 2 of 3), and relaunching the same job configuration fails the same items
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: exhausted_retry_failed_state
criteria.description: An item that exhausts its retries is marked failed with an inline error summary, while items that succeed record a simulated output, latency, and cost visible in the grid and inspector
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: virtualized_grid_scale
criteria.description: The seeded 200-plus-item job's grid scrolls smoothly during an active run, with rows rendering as they enter the viewport and no perceptible lag or blank stalls
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: progress_bar_live_fraction
criteria.description: A full-width progress bar above the grid fills proportionally as items complete and its fraction label (for example 42 of 200) updates live and matches the grid's completed count
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: pause_resume_checkpoint
criteria.description: Pausing a running job stops new items from starting and freezes progression at a checkpoint; resuming continues from exactly that checkpoint, and items completed before the pause keep their outputs, timestamps, and costs unchanged
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: retry_failed_only
criteria.description: The Retry failed items control re-queues only the failed items of the selected run — completed items keep their original outputs and timestamps and never re-execute — and the control is disabled or explains itself when no failures exist
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: optimistic_macros_reconcile
criteria.description: Start All, Pause All, and Stop All flip item statuses immediately across the grid, show a brief reconciling indicator on affected rows that settles into confirmed states, and Stop All marks pending and running items stopped without discarding completed outputs
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: queue_reorder_drag_and_keyboard
criteria.description: The pending-queue panel lists not-yet-started items in launch order, entries reorder by real drag and by keyboard-operable move up and move down controls, and after moving an item ahead it enters the running state before the items it passed
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: rollups_derive_live
criteria.description: The rollup strip shows completed count out of total, failure rate percentage, estimated time remaining, and accumulated simulated cost, all updating as items advance; pausing freezes the estimated-remaining value until resume
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: inspector_full_content
criteria.description: Clicking an item row opens an inspector with the full input text, the full simulated output, and an attempts log listing each attempt with timestamp and outcome
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: inspector_expected_diff
criteria.description: For an item whose dataset row includes an expected output, the inspector shows a comparison marking matching and differing segments between expected and actual output with distinct treatments not carried by color alone, plus a match or mismatch badge
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: run_history_and_timeline
criteria.description: Each job's run history lists runs with start timestamp and outcome counts, selecting a history entry loads that run's grid, rollups, and timeline, and the event timeline lists ordered status transitions with timestamps, filters by status, and highlights the corresponding grid row when an entry is selected
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: run_comparison_flips
criteria.description: The Compare runs view shows two selected runs of the same job side by side with one row per item, flags exactly the items whose outcomes differ with a distinct flip marker, and shows a summary count of flips that matches the flagged rows
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: schedule_form_and_badge
criteria.description: The Schedule form requires a window start and end time, rejects a missing time or an end not after the start with inline messages naming the field, and saving shows a schedule badge with the window on the job's sidebar entry that disappears when the schedule is removed
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: simulate_window_start
criteria.description: Activating Simulate window start launches each scheduled Ready job as if its window began — the job's grid begins advancing without any further manual launch
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: ics_calendar_text
criteria.description: Copy schedule as calendar text opens a monospaced block containing an ICS-format calendar (BEGIN:VCALENDAR with one VEVENT per scheduled job carrying its name and window times) and its copy control gives visible confirmation
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: export_run_report_surface
criteria.description: Export run opens a drawer or modal with a live JSON preview of the selected run plus Download JSON, Download CSV, and Copy controls; Download CSV headers include at least index, input, output, status, attempts, latencyMs, and cost
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: undo_redo_scope
criteria.description: Toolbar Undo and Redo apply to job creation, edits, deletion, queue reorders, and schedule changes, are disabled when there is nothing to undo or redo, and undoing a job deletion restores the job with its run history while redo removes it again
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: delete_confirm_and_empty_state
criteria.description: A job's Delete action opens a confirmation dialog; confirming removes the job and its runs and canceling leaves it intact, and deleting the selected job clears the main panel to an empty state with a message and a control to select or create a job
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: double_activation_guards
criteria.description: Rapidly double-activating Launch starts exactly one run (one grid fill, one new history entry), and rapidly double-activating the composer's Submit creates exactly one job
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: input_truncation
criteria.description: An input longer than 60 characters is truncated with an ellipsis in the grid row and shown in full in the inspector
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: api_shaped_create_payload_validation
criteria.description: The composer enforces the create-job field contract with inline errors naming the field: name 1 to 80 characters (over 80 names the limit), promptTemplate and model from their closed seeded sets, concurrency integer 1 through 5, dataset rows each requiring input with optional expected, and optional schedule whose windowEnd is after windowStart; Submit stays disabled until valid
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: run_report_json_schema_keys
criteria.description: The Export run JSON preview shows required keys schemaVersion exactly batch-run-v1, exportedAt, job, and run; job carries name, promptTemplate, model (atlas-40b / lyra-8b / quasar-mini / helix-2), concurrency (1 through 5), and schedule; each run.items entry carries index, input, output, status (pending / running / complete / failed / retrying / stopped), attempts, latencyMs, and cost; run.rollups carries completed, total, failureRate, estimatedRemainingMs, and totalCost
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: import_run_report_round_trip
criteria.description: Import run accepts pasted Run Report JSON matching the export field contract and restores the job configuration and run so the sidebar, grid, rollups, timeline, and next Export preview match the imported document
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: import_rejects_invalid_schema
criteria.description: Importing malformed JSON or a document that violates the field contract (schemaVersion not batch-run-v1, missing required keys, status or model outside closed sets, concurrency outside 1 through 5, name longer than 80, negative cost or attempts) shows an inline error naming the problem and leaves the current job and run unchanged
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: spacing_follows_design_scale
criteria.description: Spacing and sizing follow the design system's scale with no arbitrary one-off values visible in computed styles
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: typography_matches_spec
criteria.description: Typography matches the specified hierarchy for the app title, panel headings, and grid body text
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: layout_matches_described_composition
criteria.description: The rendered layout matches the described composition — sidebar, rollup strip with progress bar, central grid, queue and timeline alongside, inspector as a side panel, and Export run / Import run reachable from the toolbar
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: specified_state_changes_animate
criteria.description: The state changes the spec calls out — item completion, status fades, queue reorder, macro flips, modal open and close — carry their specified transitions
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: responsive_behavior_matches_spec
criteria.description: The 768 pixel sidebar collapse and the 375 pixel container-scroll behavior match the specified responsive patterns
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: component_kit_styling_consistent
criteria.description: Buttons, modals, tables, tags, and notifications share one consistent component-kit styling — radii, padding, and shadows do not vary arbitrarily between surfaces
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: heading_body_distinction
criteria.description: Headings and body text are clearly distinct in size and weight across every panel
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: component_states_match_spec
criteria.description: Default, hover, focus, disabled, and error states match the specified treatments on buttons, inputs, and toggles
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: status_and_amber_treatments_precise
criteria.description: The specified color treatments — amber concurrency saturation, red failed, green complete, amber retrying, muted stopped — render precisely and consistently
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: microinteraction_durations_match
criteria.description: Specified durations — roughly 60 millisecond item stagger, 200 to 300 millisecond modals — are observably in range
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: empty_states_designed
criteria.description: The empty main panel after deleting the selected job, the empty timeline filter result, and a queue with nothing pending each render a designed empty state with a message, not a blank region
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: forms_validate_inline
criteria.description: The composer and schedule forms validate the create-job field contract inline before submit — name 1 to 80, promptTemplate and model required, concurrency 1 through 5, at least one dataset row with input, and windowEnd after windowStart
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: errors_are_actionable
criteria.description: Parse, validation, and import errors name the problem and the offending field or rule (for example which CSV row missing input, schemaVersion batch-run-v1, concurrency 1 to 5), not just a generic rejection
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: actions_show_confirmation
criteria.description: Creating, deleting, exporting, importing, copying the calendar or report text, and completing a run each give visible success feedback such as a toast
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: simulated_work_shows_activity
criteria.description: Simulated inference shows visible activity indicators — running spinners, backoff countdowns, the reconciling indicator — never a silently frozen grid
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: destructive_actions_guarded
criteria.description: Job deletion requires confirmation and is reversible through Undo; Stop All warns or is visually distinct as destructive
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: nonobvious_controls_have_help
criteria.description: Non-obvious controls — the concurrency limit, Simulate window start, Retry failed items, the flip markers — carry tooltips or descriptive labels
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: controls_use_semantic_tags
criteria.description: Buttons, inputs, and labels across the composer, grid, and toolbar use semantic elements
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: modal_close_paths
criteria.description: Modals close via their close control, Escape, and a background click where appropriate
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: long_runs_show_progress
criteria.description: A long 200-item run continuously shows progress — the bar, fraction label, and rollups never stall visually while items advance
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: import_invalid_leaves_run_unchanged
criteria.description: Importing malformed Run Report JSON or a document that breaks the field contract shows an inline import error, leaves job count and the selected run unchanged, and does not update the Export preview as if a successful import occurred
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: field_contract_rejects_overlong_name
criteria.description: A name longer than 80 characters, concurrency outside 1 through 5, or a pasted row missing input shows an inline message naming the field and creates no job
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: delightful_microinteractions
criteria.description: Optional bonus: beyond required toasts and progress motion, the batch runner adds unique microinteractions around Launch, Export run, or retry resolution
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: advanced_motion_mechanics
criteria.description: Optional bonus: beyond the required stagger, countdown, and modal transitions, the app adds advanced motion such as coordinated flip markers or a live-drawing timeline
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: guided_onboarding
criteria.description: Optional bonus: a short coachmarks tour highlights the composer, Export run, and Launch on first visit, dismissible and never blocking the primary workflow
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: run_activity_feed
criteria.description: Optional bonus: a compact run activity feed lists the last several launches, retries, pauses, and exports with timestamps, derived from the same store as the event timeline
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: alternative_input_support
criteria.description: Optional bonus: useful keyboard shortcuts or a command palette operate Launch, Export run, or queue macros beyond the required keyboard paths
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: preference_personalization
criteria.description: Optional bonus: density toggles, column visibility, or default concurrency preferences beyond the specification are available
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: polished_product_narrative
criteria.description: Optional bonus: the app presents a polished product identity and narrative rather than an anonymous admin shell
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: dynamic_theming
criteria.description: Optional bonus: a dynamic theme or color mode beyond requirements is offered and consistently applied
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: printable_run_summary
criteria.description: Optional bonus: a printable run summary layout reachable from the export drawer restates rollups and failure rate without changing store state
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: competition_level_polish
criteria.description: Optional bonus: overall execution reaches competition-level polish visible to operators using the runner
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: innovation_catchall
criteria.description: The app demonstrates a noteworthy, browser-observable enhancement beyond the spec that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. The enhancement must plausibly matter to a real user, not be a nitpick. If present, name the enhancement and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the enhancement is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: completed_items_staggered
criteria.description: During a run started from the real Launch control, newly completed items animate into their settled state one at a time roughly 60 milliseconds apart rather than snapping together
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: progress_bar_eased
criteria.description: The progress bar advances with a smooth eased width transition rather than jumping between fractions
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: status_transition_motion
criteria.description: During a real run the running indicator shows continuous activity, a completing item's status transitions with a short fade rather than snapping, and the retry backoff countdown ticks visibly second by second
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: queue_reorder_animation
criteria.description: Reordering the pending queue through the real drag path settles the entry into its slot with a short transition, and a keyboard move slides the entry rather than teleporting it
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: macro_flip_reconcile_motion
criteria.description: Activating a real Start All, Pause All, or Stop All control transitions the affected rows to their new status together, and the reconciling indicator resolves with a subtle fade
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: hover_system
criteria.description: Buttons ease background and shadow with a slight press effect; sidebar entries, grid rows, queue entries, and timeline entries take a full-width hover wash; form controls show focus rings — verified with the real pointer while hovering
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: modal_enter_exit
criteria.description: The composer, schedule, export, import, and confirmation modals enter with a short opacity and scale transition of roughly 200 to 300 milliseconds and exit the same way
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: inspector_slide
criteria.description: The inspector panel slides in from the side when an item row is clicked and slides out when closed rather than appearing instantly
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: feedback_toasts
criteria.description: Toasts after creating, deleting, exporting, importing, copying the calendar or report text, and completing a run slide in, remain readable, and auto-dismiss with a fade
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: reduced_motion_fallback
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion set, rows and statuses appear immediately, the progress bar jumps to its current value, and transitions apply instantly while every flow stays fully usable
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: cold_start_under_2s
criteria.description: Cold start to interactive is under 2 seconds on local render, with the seeded sidebar and grid visible
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: console_clean
criteria.description: Browser devtools show no errors or warnings on load or across launch, retry, pause, macro, reorder, compare, schedule, export, import, and undo flows
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: interactions_respond_fast
criteria.description: Selecting jobs, opening the inspector, and toggling the comparison view respond in under 100 milliseconds
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: simulated_work_indicated
criteria.description: Simulated inference shows activity indicators — spinners, countdowns, the progress bar — throughout the run
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: large_grid_renders_smoothly
criteria.description: The 200-plus-item grid scrolls and updates without perceived lag while the run is active
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: ui_interactive_during_run
criteria.description: The UI stays interactive during an active run — the inspector opens, the queue reorders, and jobs switch while items advance
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: animations_hold_frame_rate
criteria.description: Row settles, the progress bar, and macro flips hold a smooth frame rate with the large job running
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: rapid_input_never_freezes
criteria.description: Rapid macro clicks, quick job switches, and fast timeline filtering never hang or freeze the app
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: extended_session_stable
criteria.description: Running, retrying, and comparing across an extended session shows no runaway slowdown or memory growth symptoms
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: long_work_degrades_gracefully
criteria.description: Pausing or stopping mid-run leaves a responsive, coherent UI rather than a stuck busy state
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: layout_adapts_1440_to_375
criteria.description: The layout adapts gracefully from 1440 pixel desktop to 375 pixel mobile — sidebar, rollups, grid, queue, and inspector all remain usable
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: mobile_tap_targets
criteria.description: Macro buttons, queue move controls, row actions, and sidebar entries are tap targets of at least 44 pixels at mobile widths
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: typography_scales
criteria.description: Headings, grid text, and rollup values resize appropriately between desktop and mobile
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: no_viewport_clipping
criteria.description: No rollup, grid, queue, or inspector content clips or overflows the viewport at any width between 1440 and 375 pixels
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: sidebar_collapses_at_768
criteria.description: At widths of 768 pixels and below the job sidebar collapses behind a toggle that opens it as an overlay; at desktop widths it is open by default
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: stacking_reflows_logically
criteria.description: At narrow widths the rollup strip, grid, queue, and timeline stack in a logical reading order
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: touch_paths_work
criteria.description: On a touch viewport, tapping rows, macros, and queue move controls works; queue reordering remains achievable via the move controls even if drag is unavailable
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: no_page_horizontal_scroll
criteria.description: At 375 pixel width no page-level horizontal scrolling appears; the execution grid, comparison view, and export JSON preview scroll within their own containers
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: grid_sizes_responsively
criteria.description: The virtualized grid and comparison table size to their containers rather than forcing a fixed width
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: fixed_controls_reachable
criteria.description: The toolbar, undo/redo, and macro controls remain reachable at every width
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: shared_state_coherence
criteria.description: All surfaces derive from one shared state: an item advancing updates the grid, progress bar, rollup strip, and event timeline together; a retry, pause, resume, or macro is reflected in the grid, rollups, history, and any open inspector; and a job edit, delete, or undo is reflected everywhere the job appears, all without a reload
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: no_storage_seeded_reload
criteria.description: localStorage and sessionStorage remain empty after composing, launching, retrying, pausing, reordering, scheduling, exporting, importing, and undoing, and a page reload returns the app to its seeded state (seeded jobs and history, default view, no schedules, closed export/import)
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: rapid_input_keeps_sync
criteria.description: Rapid job switches, macro clicks, and timeline filtering while the 200-item job runs leave every surface consistent — no view shows a status or count another surface already updated differently
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: all_views_reachable
criteria.description: Every documented surface — composer, grid, queue, inspector, timeline, comparison, schedule form, Export run, Import run — is reachable through in-app controls and renders without errors
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: compose_flow_lands_everywhere
criteria.description: Composing a job from pasted CSV creates a sidebar entry whose name, row count, and Ready status match the composer input, and the job is immediately launchable
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: invalid_compose_inline_validation
criteria.description: Submitting the composer with an empty name or zero dataset rows triggers immediate inline validation naming the field, with no job created
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: edit_flow_updates_displays
criteria.description: Editing a job (name, concurrency, or schedule) updates its sidebar entry, composer prefill, and any run headers without a reload
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: delete_flow_clears_surfaces
criteria.description: Deleting a job removes it from the sidebar, the comparison pickers, and the main panel after confirmation
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: run_view_switches_retain_state
criteria.description: Switching between the grid, comparison view, and another job and back retains the run's statuses, the applied timeline filter, and the queue order
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: last_delete_reveals_empty_state
criteria.description: Deleting every job leaves a clear empty state in the sidebar and main panel with a control to create a new job
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: timeline_filter_flow
criteria.description: Filtering the event timeline by status narrows entries everywhere the timeline renders, and clearing the filter restores the full log
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: sidebar_collapse_continuity
criteria.description: Collapsing and reopening the job sidebar (including the overlay at narrow widths) preserves the selected job and run state
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: overlays_support_flows
criteria.description: The composer, schedule form, confirmation dialog, and inspector each open, complete their flow, and close without losing surrounding state
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: recovery_without_reload
criteria.description: A mis-step — canceling a compose, closing the inspector, clearing a comparison — recovers to a working view without a reload or dead end
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: durability_pipeline_end_to_end
criteria.description: The full durable pipeline works in one session: compose with create-payload fields, launch, deterministic failures retry with countdowns, one item exhausts into failed, Retry failed items resolves it, a pause and resume continues from the checkpoint, and Export run JSON shows schemaVersion batch-run-v1 with job/model/concurrency and per-item statuses, attempts, and costs matching the grid exactly
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: mutation_to_export_flow
criteria.description: After Stop All or a retry, reopening Export run shows the JSON items array and rollups reflecting those mutations, with Copy and Download JSON available
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: export_import_round_trip_flow
criteria.description: After a run mutation, Copy or Download the Run Report JSON, Import that same text, and confirm the visible grid, rollups, and timeline match the pre-export mutated state and the next Export preview matches again
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: workspace_layout_composition
criteria.description: The app composes as a left job sidebar and a main area with the rollup strip and progress bar on top, the execution grid in the center, and the inspector as a side panel, with the pending queue and event timeline alongside the grid while a run is active or selected; Export run and Import run are reachable from the toolbar
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: status_color_system
criteria.description: Item statuses are visually distinct at a glance — pending neutral, running with a blue active indicator, retrying amber, failed red, complete green, stopped muted — and the same treatment per status appears in the grid, pending queue, timeline, and comparison view
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: concurrency_strip_states
criteria.description: The concurrency guard strip renders calm below the limit and switches to a clearly amber treatment while the running count equals the limit
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: consistent_currency_format
criteria.description: Costs render with one consistent currency format (dollar sign and a fixed number of decimal places) across the grid, rollup strip, inspector, and exports
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: active_job_highlight
criteria.description: The selected sidebar job carries a selected-layer background visibly distinct from the hover treatment of other entries
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: typography_hierarchy
criteria.description: Typography shows a clear hierarchy — the app title larger than panel headings, which are larger than grid body and label text — consistent across views
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: spacing_rhythm
criteria.description: Gaps between the sidebar, rollup strip, grid, queue, and inspector are visually regular with no crowded or orphaned regions
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: control_state_treatments
criteria.description: Buttons, inputs, selects, and toggles show distinct default, hover, focus (visible ring), disabled, and error treatments
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: single_icon_set
criteria.description: One consistent icon set is used throughout the toolbar, sidebar entries, status cells, and row actions
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: ics_monospace_block
criteria.description: The ICS calendar text and the Run Report JSON preview each render in a monospaced block visually distinct from surrounding UI text
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: headings_consistent_capitalization
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings and panel titles (job sidebar, rollups, queue, timeline), they use one consistent capitalization convention throughout
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: actions_use_specific_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders action labels, they are specific verbs — such as Launch, Retry failed items, Export run, Import run, Copy, Download — not generic Submit or OK when a specific label is possible
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: errors_name_problem_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders errors — malformed paste, schedule time order, empty dataset, invalid import schemaVersion — the message names the field and the rule broken, not just a rejection
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: empty_states_explain_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders empty states (no jobs, no timeline matches, nothing to retry), the copy explains what belongs there and how to proceed
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: terminology_is_consistent
criteria.description: The same concepts keep one name across surfaces — job, run, item, retry, schedule — not job here and batch there
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: numbers_dates_units_consistent
criteria.description: Costs, latencies, timestamps, and fractions use consistent formats and labeled units across the grid, rollups, inspector, and exports
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.

criteria.name: success_messages_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders confirmations, they state what happened — such as Job created or Report downloaded — not vague affirmations
criteria.reasoning: Verified through UI or code inspection that it works.
